bullets.text: Oil prices surged to $120 a barrel amid reports of an extended Iranian blockade, impacting global aid efforts and preventing the delivery of essential supplies to millions in need.
Russia has scaled back its annual Victory Day parade in Moscow, citing threats from Ukraine, and will not feature military vehicles or cadets this year.
Fears of a resurgence in Somali piracy have emerged after three vessels were hijacked in a week, as international naval forces are reportedly diverted to the Middle East.
US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth defended the war against Iran in his first testimony to Congress, as the conflict's cost to the US is estimated at $25 billion.
Investigations are underway into a light plane crash at an airport in Adelaide's north that resulted in two fatalities and injured 10 others.
A suspect has been arrested in north London following the stabbing of two Jewish men.
The Federal Reserve is reportedly divided on monetary policy amid escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, with oil prices surging and casting a shadow on the economic outlook.

Meta's shares have dropped following an announcement of increased AI spending, while Google, Microsoft, and Amazon report strong growth in their cloud computing divisions, indicating a competitive landscape in the tech sector.
BP and Venezuela have signed an agreement to explore for offshore natural gas, signaling a potential revival in Venezuela's energy sector and increased global interest in its resources.
